title: Overcut-vs-Undercut Regime Experts
summary: Official F1 strategy analysis shows Monaco-like low-energy tracks can favor overcuts because new tyres warm slowly, while high-degradation venues favor earlier undercuts.
rationale: Official F1 strategy analysis shows Monaco-like low-energy tracks can favor overcuts because new tyres warm slowly, while high-degradation venues favor earlier undercuts. That implies the sign of some interactions changes by circuit regime, which a single global tree model may blur.
implementation_hint: Define a small set of race regimes from `Race` and simple priors: low-warmup/track-position circuits, high-degradation/undercut circuits, and one-stop-biased circuits. Train separate boosters or a gated mixture-of-experts, then OOF-blend them with the baseline model.
expected_effect: Should reduce race-shift error by letting the model learn different meanings for tyre age, lap delta, and stint depth across track types.
risk: Only 26 races are available, so too many regimes or overly bespoke rules will overfit fast.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
